title: Allow push to use the image id
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

Allows this command to work:
```
./buildah push bea0ba3aafbf oci:/tmp/image:test
```
where  bea0ba3aafbf is the ImageId for the image.  Prior when we passed 'image' to ParseStoreReference it would package up the image name as "docker.io/library/bea0ba3aafbf:latest" rather than "docker.io/library/alpine:latest" which is the valid name.

At line 312 of the commit.go file below we do:

builder, err := importBuilderFromImage(options.Store, importOptions)

The value returned from that in the builder.FromImage variable is "docker.io/library/alpine:latest" which ParseStoreReference parses appropriately and the push now succeeds.

Fixes #324

comments.body: Do we have a test for this?
No tests that use the imageid, the ones in play currently use just the short or long name.  I'll see if I can work one up.
Dang, I have to stop hitting the close button.
It looks like make has to be added to buildah.spec as a requirement, since fedora:26 no longer ships it by default?
Hmm, this was all green prior to adding the test, I'm going to close and reopen to see if it works.  If not I'll look into the make change in the *.spec file.
@TomSweeneyRedHat I think the Base image has changed and dropped the make command. so we need to make sure the buildah.spec file includes a BuildRequires make.
Yeah I think you 're right about the base image.  I must of just had bad timing as it failed again.  Will update the spec after I get through e-mail/reviews.  Thx for the tip.

:pushpin: Commit b9e37fa has been approved by `nalind`
